---
title: "environmental_covariates"
author: "Luke LeBar"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

# Pulling Environmental Covariates from T3

## Loading Packages

```{r}
rm(list=ls())
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(here))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(BrAPI))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(purrr))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidyverse))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(here))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tidygeocoder))
suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(nasapower))
```

## Load T3 Trial Metadata

```{r}
#setwd("C:/Users/lukel/OneDrive/Documents/GitHub/Predictathon/data")
here::i_am("analysis/environmental_covariates.Rmd")
trials <- read.csv(here("analysis", "trial_list.csv"))
```

## Geocode Locations

```{r}
trials_geocoded <- trials %>%
  geocode(address = location, method = 'osm', lat = latitude, long = longitude)
if(any(is.na(trials_geocoded$latitude))) {
  warning("Some locations could not be geocoded automatically.")
}
```

## Retrieve Environmental Data from NASA

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(daymetr)

fetch_daymet <- function(lat, lon, year) {
  
  # Notice we are using the lowercase 'year' here to match the argument above
  dl <- download_daymet(
    site = "Site",  
    lat = lat, 
    lon = lon, 
    start = year,   
    end = year,     
    internal = TRUE, 
    silent = TRUE 
  )
  
  return(dl$data)
}

# 2. Your Pipeline (Uppercase 'YEAR' is correct here because it's your column name)
trials_geocoded <- trials_geocoded %>%
  mutate(weather_data = pmap(list(latitude, longitude, YEAR), fetch_daymet))


trials_env <- env_results %>%
  tidyr::unnest(weather_data, names_repair = "unique")

trials_env <- trials_env[,-10]

trials_env

write.csv(trials_env, "environmental_covariates.csv")
```
